creators.name: Nick Saraev
creators.blurb: Practical AI automation and agency builds from someone shipping real systems.

videos.title: I Built a $1M/y SaaS with Claude Code, Here's How
videos.summary: Nick Saraev describes building Clairvo, a $1M/year SaaS that uses Claude Code-generated techniques like predictive pacing and local number rotation to improve call pickup rates. He argues that using Claude to enumerate opportunities and practical implementation of AI agents is viable for building real-world revenue-generating businesses, contrasting his approach with many who talk automation without results.

videos.title: How to Build Mobile Apps with Claude Code: Full Course (2026)
videos.summary: Nick Saraev's full-length course teaches mobile app development using Claude Code and Expo/React Native. The course covers setup, app design frameworks, building three complete apps (habit tracker, calorie tracker with AI, Pomodoro timer), database integration with Supabase, user authentication, and app store submission.

videos.title: How to Use Claude Code for FREE (2026)
videos.summary: Nick Saraev demonstrates how to use Claude Code for free or at minimal cost by routing through NVIDIA-NIM, OpenRouter, Ollama for local models, and alternatives like DeepSeek V4 Flash. The video includes setup instructions for each method and references a full 4-hour Claude Code course.

videos.title: Claude Opus-4.7 Just Dropped, And...
videos.summary: Claude Opus-4.7 is the latest version of Anthropic's Claude model. Nick Saraev discusses the release and its applications in a YouTube video covering practical automation workflows and business use cases.

creators.name: AI Engineer
creators.blurb: Talks and sessions from the AI Engineer conference and community.

videos.title: You Might Not Need 50 Diffusion Steps — Ziv Ilan, Nvidia
videos.summary: Ziv Ilan's team at NVIDIA demonstrated real-time video diffusion on Blackwell B200 by combining dynamic quantization, latent caching, and step distillation to reduce diffusion models from 50 steps to as few as one. The technique stacks three layers: quantization with Black Forest Labs on Flux 2, skip-caching that avoids recomputing unchanged latent chunks, and distillation using either trajectory-based or distribution-based student training approaches.

videos.title: Why MCP and ChatGPT Apps Use Double Iframes — Frédéric Barthelet, Alpic
videos.summary: ChatGPT apps and MCP use double iframe architecture to isolate third-party code while preserving functionality. The outer iframe loads from a controlled subdomain with a lightweight script, which then loads the actual app HTML via srcdoc into an inner frame, preventing CSP breaches and sandbox escapes while blocking cross-app storage collisions.

videos.title: Why Can't Anyone Answer Questions About the Business? — Garrett Galow, WorkOS
videos.summary: Garrett Galow from WorkOS built Studio, an internal workspace where non-technical users can ask natural language questions against Snowflake, Linear, and Notion data sources. The system generates reusable declarative JavaScript widgets that call data sources directly, making subsequent runs deterministic and cost-efficient while eliminating repetitive SQL request loops.

videos.title: The agent-ready web: Simplify user actions with WebMCP — Tara Agyemang, Google
videos.summary: WebMCP is a Google Chrome standard proposal that enables AI agents to interact with websites through structured, typed tool interfaces instead of DOM parsing and pixel coordinate calculation. It offers both declarative HTML attributes and imperative JavaScript APIs for registering custom tools, with early preview in Chrome 146.

creators.name: Cole Medin
creators.blurb: Practical AI agent and coding-assistant builds, end to end.

videos.title: Omnigent: The New Meta-Harness for EVERY Coding Agent - Claude Code, Codex, Pi, More
videos.summary: Omnigent is an open-source meta-harness that enables orchestration of multiple coding agents (Claude Code, Codex, Pi) as interchangeable workers. It provides task planning, implementation routing, code review workflows, human-in-the-loop governance, and cross-device session management for coordinated AI-assisted coding.

videos.title: Claude Fable 5 is Now BANNED?!
videos.summary: Cole Medin discusses the sudden withdrawal of Anthropic's Claude Fable 5 model following a US government export-control directive citing a claimed jailbreak. The video covers reasons for the pulldown, impact on customers, and performance benchmarks comparing Fable 5 to Claude Opus 4.8.

videos.title: Google's Agents CLI: The CLI + Skills Combination to Ship AI Agents EASILY
videos.summary: Google's Agents CLI is an open-source command-line tool that enables rapid building and deployment of AI agents to production. It provides agent identity, sandboxed code execution, and audit trails for production-grade AI agent systems.

videos.title: Live AI Q&A + Crushing it in Chess at the Same Time - Come Hang Out!
videos.summary: Cole Medin is hosting a live Q&A stream on Saturday focused on AI coding, agents, Claude Code, and Archon. The stream will combine Q&A discussion with live chess gameplay and is part of regular weekend content for the Dynamous community.

creators.name: AI Jason
creators.blurb: AI app and agent builds, experiments, and walkthroughs.

videos.title: How to build proactive agents & self-improving company (Fully explained)
videos.summary: Video explains how to build proactive AI agents and self-improving company systems through autonomous loops. Covers use cases including autonomous ads optimization and SEO with supporting tools like Loopany for memory and tooling.

videos.title: Ralph-loop 2.0? The real autonomous coder is coming...
videos.summary: AI Jason discusses emerging autonomous coding capabilities, highlighting features like /goal directives and multi-step task exploration. The video covers practical setup and prompting strategies for achieving longer-horizon autonomous coding workflows.

videos.title: New AI coding paradiagm - OpenAI Symphony
videos.summary: OpenAI Symphony is presented as a new AI coding paradigm for integrating AI agents into software development workflows. The video covers setup, harness configuration, automated testing, and workflow orchestration with Symphony.

videos.title: Okay, this unleashed my agent
videos.summary: AI Jason reviews autonomous agent implementations including Autoresearch, AutoAgent, and Crewlet. The video covers a 3-layer memory system in Claude Code, self-evolution mechanisms in Hermes and Openclaw, and how these enable agents to tackle complex tasks.

creators.name: Caleb Writes Code
creators.blurb: Hands-on coding sessions and software build-alongs.

videos.title: NVIDIA’s Nemotron 3 Is... Awesome?
videos.summary: NVIDIA released the Nemotron 3 series with three model sizes: Nano, Super, and Ultra. The models employ a hybrid architecture combining Mamba transformers, Latent MoE, and MTP techniques to differentiate themselves from other open-source models.

videos.title: MiniMax M3 explained in 8min..
videos.summary: MiniMax M3 is a language model that shifts from full attention to sparse attention (MSA). It includes tiled attention, optimized KV cache with continuous reads, and I/O improvements to reduce latency during prefill and decode stages of inference.

videos.title: Pi Agent explained in 6min..
videos.summary: Caleb Writes Code explains Pi Agent, an agent framework that can be harnessed in unique ways. The video compares Pi against frontier agents like Claude Code, Codex, Cursor, and Antigravity, discussing how harness layers become more complex as products add features.

videos.title: Huawei makes 1.4nm chip??
videos.summary: Caleb Eom discusses Huawei's announcement of Tau Scaling Law as a competitive response in the hardware and semiconductor race between China and the USA. The piece examines the roles of EUV and DUV lithography, packaging, and logic folding in advancing chip manufacturing capabilities.

creators.name: AWS Developers
creators.blurb: Official AWS tutorials, service deep dives, and developer demos.

videos.title: Agent Toolkit for AWS Explained in 2 Minutes
videos.summary: Agent Toolkit for AWS is a framework that equips AI coding agents with tools, current documentation, best practices, and guardrails for interacting with AWS services. It improves agent success rates by providing verified procedures instead of relying on outdated or incomplete general knowledge.

videos.title: Running Claude Code on Amazon Bedrock
videos.summary: Claude Code is an agentic coding tool by Anthropic that runs on Amazon Bedrock, enabling developers to build features, fix bugs, and automate tasks while keeping data within AWS infrastructure for compliance and data residency requirements. The guide covers environment setup, cross-region inference, prompt caching, the Bedrock Mantle endpoint, cost tracking, and Guardrails for content governance.

videos.title: AI and the Job Market in 2026
videos.summary: AWS Developers discusses how AI is reshaping tech job hunting in 2026, emphasizing that networking and personal branding outperform mass applications. The video argues junior developers remain valuable despite AI automation, drawing parallels to how transportation evolved rather than disappeared, and recommends building projects, blogging, and open-source contributions to stand out.

videos.title: 5 Tips for Deploying AI Agents to Production
videos.summary: AWS Developers outlines five production best practices for deploying TypeScript AI agents: streaming tool call events for UI responsiveness, layered authentication with API Gateway and Cognito, type-safe contracted database access with tenant isolation, guardrails and cost budgets via lifecycle hooks, and observability through metrics and OpenTelemetry tracing. The video demonstrates these patterns using the Strands Agents SDK on AWS infrastructure.

creators.name: Visual AI
creators.blurb: Visual walkthroughs and demos across modern AI tools.

creators.name: Alberta Tech
creators.blurb: Developer tutorials and practical builds in AI and software.

videos.title: Do Google engineers actually vibe code?
videos.summary: A video discussion examining whether Google engineers use informal or intuitive coding practices, with comparisons to code generation capabilities from OpenAI Codex and Claude. The content covers current vibe coding trends at Google spanning 2024 to 2026.

videos.title: This company killed open source
videos.summary: A YouTube video discussing how a company called Malus is changing the open source landscape, featuring commentary on open source maintainers, AI agents, and the future of the open source model. The video covers concerns around open source sustainability and explores whether Malus represents a threat or solution.

videos.title: I let Claude Code take my job (it did better than me)
videos.summary: Alberta Tech conducted an experiment comparing Claude Code's software development performance against their own work and found the AI outperformed them. The video explores whether AI tools like Claude Code will replace human developers across all job functions.

creators.name: EO
creators.blurb: Conversations and stories with founders and builders in tech.

videos.title: "ChatGPT Moment" for Robotics Is Coming. The Real Problem Isn't Intelligence | Stanford, Catie Cuan
videos.summary: Catie Cuan argues that the upcoming wave of robots should prioritize design for delight and human interaction rather than pursuing humanoid form factors and pure utility. She critiques the humanoid robot race led by companies like 1X and Unitree as a myopic vision, suggesting this narrow focus misses broader opportunities for robot design and societal integration.

videos.title: How to Steal Customers From Giants 600x Bigger than Your Startup | Pigment, Eléonore Crespo
videos.summary: Eléonore Crespo, co-founder of Pigment, discusses how to compete against established giants through full platform development, enterprise trust strategies, and disciplined hiring practices. The interview covers why large companies like Google use spreadsheets for planning and how newer startups can gain credibility with marquee customers.

videos.title: The Fastest Way to Know if Your Product Market Fit Is Real | Serval CEO, Jake Stauch
videos.summary: Jake Stauch, CEO of Serval, argues that passionate early adopters do not prove real product-market fit and that true PMF manifests as customer pull rather than founder push. He recommends embedded customer relationships over formal interviews and advises betting on AI model trajectory rather than current capabilities.

videos.title: He Raised $26M Betting on Probabilities, Not Guesses | Koah Labs, Nic Baird
videos.summary: Nic Baird, founder of Koah Labs, argues that ad-based monetization rather than subscriptions is the future for AI tools, having raised $26M on this thesis. He identifies human trust adoption, not technology, as the primary bottleneck limiting AI agent deployment.

creators.name: Cyril Imhof
creators.blurb: AI automation and workflow builds for real projects.

videos.title: Claude Fable 5 with Harness Engineering & Loops Will Do Everything.
videos.summary: Cyril Imhof discusses Claude Fable 5 capabilities with focus on harness engineering and loops for AI automation. Video covers open source versus closed source AI model considerations, risks of relying on proprietary models, and implications of model shutdowns for regulated industries.

videos.title: Claude Fable 5 Now Banned in Europe by US Government (EU-Breakdown)
videos.summary: A video by Cyril Imhof discusses the claimed ban of Claude Fable 5 in Europe by the US government. The video references regulatory developments concerning Anthropic's model access across EU jurisdictions.

videos.title: The Ultimate Second Brain for AI Entrepreneurs: Fable 5 Claude with Loops
videos.summary: Cyril Imhof discusses Fable 5, an Anthropic model designed for AI entrepreneurs, highlighting its features, guardrails, memory capabilities, and agentic payment integration compared to Mythos 5.

videos.title: Anthropic's Brand New AI Just Started the Gold Rush (Fable 5)
videos.summary: Cyril Imhof discusses Anthropic's new Claude Fable 5 model and its applications in regulated industries. The video covers new use cases for the model and introduces "Know Your Agent" methodology for effective context engineering in long-running AI applications.

creators.name: PY
creators.blurb: Software engineering, tooling, and developer-workflow deep dives.
